events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need decide pass/fail. Scenario: residential job, automotive brakes, observed field condition: caliper guide pins dry/seized. Work rough-complete. Need assess compliance with OEM specs, FMVSS-adjacent shop practice. Caliper guide pins must be lubricated and free-moving for proper brake operation. Dry/seized guide pins cause uneven pad wear, reduced braking, potential failure. Non-compliant and installed-defective. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Seized guide pins can cause brake pull, overheating, premature failure, safety critical. In residential job maybe high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 80? Value not asked 0. Sound_source etc default empty string/0. Workable empty. Findings: caliper guide pins lack lubrication and are seized; improper assembly. Actions: remove caliper, clean and lubricate guide pins with approved brake grease, inspect pins and boots, replace if damaged, verify caliper slides freely, retorque per spec, test brakes. Rationale: OEM and FMVSS require caliper guide pins to be properly lubricated and move freely to ensure balanced braking and prevent seizure; dry/seized pins are a defect and non-compliant. References: OEM brake service specs, FMVSS 135 (hydraulic brake systems) performance requirements, industry shop practice. Need valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

  "findings": [
    "Caliper guide pins are dry and seized.",
    "Guide pins were not properly lubricated during assembly.",
    "Seized pins prevent free caliper movement and can cause uneven braking, pull, overheating, and accelerated pad wear."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the caliper and extract the guide pins.",
    "Clean all corrosion and old debris from pins and bores.",
    "Inspect guide pins, boots, and bushings for damage; replace any worn or damaged parts.",
    "Apply manufacturer-approved high-temperature brake caliper grease to the guide pins.",
    "Reinstall boots correctly to keep out moisture and contaminants.",
    "Verify the caliper slides freely and re-torque fasteners to OEM specification.",
    "Reassemble brakes, bleed hydraulic system if opened, and perform a brake function test before returning to service."
  ],
  "rationale": "Caliper guide pins must remain clean, lubricated, and free to move so the caliper can self-center and apply braking force evenly. Dry/seized guide pins are an assembly defect that violates OEM service requirements and FMVSS-adjacent safe brake practice, creating a safety hazard.",
  "references": [
    "OEM brake service specifications",
    "FMVSS 135 / safe brake system performance practice",
    "Industry standard brake reassembly and lubrication practice"
  ]
}
